The original test case incorrectly passes 'axis' and 'descending' as call-time arguments, causing 'multiple values for argument axis' error. Also, MindSpore does not support float64 for Sort on all backends (only float16, int types are well supported; float32 may have accuracy loss, float64 is unsupported). Therefore, dtype must be downgraded to float32 or float16. Since test environment is unspecified but error is 'multiple values for axis', the root cause is misuse of API signature, not backend limitation. Repair: (1) fix usage pattern — separate instantiation and call; (2) change dtype to float32 (widely supported); (3) ensure input is passed as positional 'x' in call.
